howItWorks.intro: This triangle area calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is the easiest way to find triangle area?
faq.answer: The simplest formula is A = ½ × base × height. Multiply the base by the perpendicular height and divide by 2.

faq.question: How does Heron's formula work?
faq.answer: Heron's formula finds the area when you know all three sides. First find the semi-perimeter s = (a+b+c)/2, then compute A = √[s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)].

faq.question: When do I use the two-sides-and-angle method?
faq.answer: Use A = ½ab·sin(C) when you know two sides and the angle between them (the included angle). This is the SAS (Side-Angle-Side) area method.

faq.question: Can a triangle have zero area?
faq.answer: A triangle has zero area only when the three vertices are collinear (all on the same line). Any three non-collinear points form a triangle with positive area.
